activities.name: British Museum
activities.description: World-class museum with free admission. Rosetta Stone, Elgin Marbles, Egyptian mummies.

activities.name: Tower of London
activities.description: Historic castle, Crown Jewels, Yeoman Warder tours. Nearly 1,000 years of royal history.

activities.name: Borough Market Food Tour
activities.description: London's premier food market since 1756. Artisan cheeses, pastries, global street food.

activities.name: Westminster Walking Tour
activities.description: Big Ben, Houses of Parliament, Westminster Abbey, St James's Park, Buckingham Palace.

activities.name: Tate Modern
activities.description: Free modern art museum in a converted power station. Turbine Hall installations.

activities.name: Camden Market & Camden Town
activities.description: Eclectic markets, street food from 30+ countries, live music, and vintage finds.

activities.name: St. Stephen's Hall
activities.description: Upon entry, you pass through a metal detector, and are very thoroughly searched. You then proceed into St. Stephen's Hall, where you are seated to wait for admission. A representative of the Sergeant-at-Arms gives you a

activities.name: House of Commons Strangers' Gallery
activities.description: When called, you proceed from St. Stephen's Hall to the Central Hall, and then upstairs. You must leave all items (bags, cameras, mobile phones, writing and written material) outside and then proceed through to the Stran

activities.name: House of Lords Strangers' Gallery
activities.description: If you head away from the Commons, you pass along a corridor towards the Lords. If you ask to visit the Strangers' Gallery, a representative of Black Rod asks you to complete another slip of paper with your name and addr

activities.name: Westminster Hall
activities.description: After visiting the two Houses, visitors pass back through St. Stephen's Hall, and through Westminster Hall. Westminster Hall is one of the few areas of Parliament in which photography is permitted, and it is a very impre

activities.name: State Opening of Parliament
activities.description: Probably the most colourful event in the UK's legislative calendar, this takes place in May or June every year, or after a general election, when the King travels to the Houses of Parliament to open the new parliamentary

activities.name: Summer Opening
activities.description: While the Houses are in recess, the Palace of Westminster is generally closed - apart from the long Summer recess, during which tours are run through the building, led by Parliamentary employees. Popularity of these tour

activities.name: Elizabeth Tower
activities.description: Strictly limited numbers of tickets are available to climb the Elizabeth Tower, which was renamed from Clock Tower in 2012. It contains the Great Bell, commonly known as Big Ben. British visitors should write to their Me

activities.name: The Jewel Tower
activities.description: This small tower across the road from the Houses of Parliament is the only part of the original Palace of Westminster still standing. While it is overshadowed in splendour by the surrounding buildings, it's well worth a

activities.name: Wellington Arch
activities.location: Apsley Way, Hyde Park Corner, W1J 7JZ

activities.name: Duke of Wellington
activities.description: Equestrian statue of the Iron Duke, with four soldiers at each corner of the pedestal, representing regiments that fought under him: a Grenadier, Scottish Highlander, Irish Dragoon, and Welsh Fusilier. The bronze came fr

activities.name: Royal Artillery Memorial
activities.description: A stone howitzer by World War I veteran Charles Sargeant Jagger

activities.name: Australian War Memorial
activities.description: Created by Tonkin Zulaikha Greer and Janet Laurence to remember the Australians who died in both world wars. Made from Australian granite.

activities.name: Machine Gun Corps Memorial
activities.description: A statue of David holding Goliath's sword by Francis Derwent Wood commemorating the dead of the World War I corps.

activities.name: New Zealand Memorial
activities.description: Sixteen cruciform bronze pillars designed by Paul Dibble and John Hardwick-Smith.

activities.name: RAF Bomber Command Memorial
activities.location: Green Park, on the south side of Piccadilly close to Hyde Park Corner. Tube: {{station

activities.name: Thomas Cubitt
activities.location: Corner of Denbigh St and St George's Drive

activities.name: Cabinet War Rooms and Churchill Museum
activities.location: Clive Steps, King Charles St, SW1A 2AQ

activities.name: Downing Street
activities.description: Site of the London residences for the Prime Minister (No. 10) and the Chancellor (No. 11).

activities.name: Changing of the Guard
activities.description: Each morning between May and July at 11:30AM the guard changes outside Buckingham Palace. The rest of the year, the guard changes on alternate days, weather permitting. A board is placed outside the palace in the morning

activities.name: Dismounting Ceremony
activities.description: In the late afternoon at Horse Guards an officer will inspect the guards, after which the mounted sentries return their horses to the stables for the night. They are replaced by two dismounted sentries. This ceremony was

activities.name: Trooping the Colour
activities.description: A ceremony performed by regiments of the British and Commonwealth armies to mark the official birthday of the King. The King and his family travel by carriage down The Mall from Buckingham Palace to Horse Guards Parade,

activities.name: Major General's Review
activities.description: Tickets are made available by the same ballot as Trooping the Colour.

activities.name: Colonel's Review
activities.description: Tickets are made available by the same ballot as Trooping the Colour.

activities.name: Beating Retreat
activities.description: This began as a 16th-century tradition for recalling troops to a castle at sunset before the gates closed. Today, the ceremony has evolved into a military pageant of music and precision drilling, while also serving as pr

activities.name: Cineworld Haymarket
activities.description: Slightly off the main area for cinemas and entertainment around Leicester Square and Theatreland this cinema offers projections of recent movies on three large screens.

activities.name: Vue Piccadilly
activities.description: Multi screen of the Vue network very close to Piccadilly Circus.

activities.name: St John's Smith Square
activities.description: Former baroque church by Thomas Archer. From the 1960s, converted into a concert hall and featuring world class classical music performers with daily concerts from September to July.
activities.location: St John's Smith Square, London, SW1P 3HA

activities.name: Free Walking Tours
activities.description: There are a number of regular free walking tours in London but the most well known leaves from The Duke of Wellingon Arch twice daily and covers many of the important sights in Westminster. Duration about 2½ hours.

activities.name: Budd Shirts
activities.description: Budd, established in 1910, is the only Jermyn Street shirtmaker to still have a bespoke cutting room on site, where shirts are still made.

activities.name: Westminster Cathedral Cafe
activities.description: The cafe in the basement of Westminster Cathedral is one of the cheapest places to have lunch in the area. Simple tasty hot lunches (Jacket Potatoes etc) cost under £5. Get in through the Cathedral main entrance.

activities.name: The Laughing Halibut
activities.description: The classic shop for fish and chips.

activities.name: Pataka
activities.description: Opened in 2021, Pataka serves modern Indian food.
activities.location: Grosvenor Gardens House, 50-52 Buckingham Palace Rd, SW1W 0RN

activities.name: The Ebury Restaurant and Wine Bar
Zizzi
activities.description: A cozy Italian restaurant next to the Westminster Cathedral and Victoria Station, with a huge wood fired pizza oven that creates a welcome atmosphere on its own. Pizzas between £12 - £16, and various pasta dishes and sal

activities.name: The Cinnamon Club
activities.description: Indian restaurant. Housed in a converted Grade II listed library.
activities.location: The Old Westminster Library, 30–32 Great Smith St, SW1P 3BU

activities.name: Wiltons
activities.description: Oyster bar, game and seafood restaurant. Traditional fine English dining in a restaurant established in 1742.

activities.name: London Dungeon
activities.location: County Hall, Westminster Bridge Rd, SE1 7PB

activities.name: OXO Tower
activities.description: Home to designer-marker boutiques, selling everything from hats and homeware to wedding dresses and jewellery. Modern gallery on the ground floor which is free and open every day, various cafes, a florists and hairdresse

activities.name: Winchester Palace
activities.location: Corner of Clink St and Storey St

activities.name: Millennium Bridge
activities.description: This steel pedestrian bridge connecting Bankside with the City of London has all the lovely views of the Thames that you'd expect, but most of those who cross Millennium Bridge never notice its most distinctive feature.

activities.name: Hayward Gallery
activities.description: The largest public art space in the UK and the exhibitions showcase masters and young British and international artists, usually accompanied by outdoor installations that bring the area to life. The gallery often holds f

activities.name: Fashion & Textile Museum
activities.description: Founded by veteran designer Zandra Rhodes.

activities.name: Florence Nightingale Museum
activities.location: St Thomas' Hospital, Westminster Bridge Rd

activities.name: Namco Funscape
activities.location: County Hall, Westminster Bridge Rd, SE1 7PB

activities.name: Frog Tour
activities.description: Along the Thames and through the streets of London in an amphibious vehicle. Operated by Capital Frogs Ltd, County Hall, on the south side of Westminster Bridge.

activities.name: Borough Market
activities.description: One of London's favourite wholesale working food markets, on Fridays and Saturdays offering a general retail market with specialties sold directly from the producers, organic products, delicatessen, cheese, wines, fish a

activities.name: Gabriel's Wharf
activities.description: An eclectic mix of shops and restaurants, the design shops at Gabriel's Wharf are exclusively run by small businesses who design and manufacture their own products, the majority of work available will have been made by t

activities.name: Hay's Galleria
activities.description: A beautiful converted wharf on the Jubilee Walk which, since 1987, has been home to cafés, shops and traditional barrows. Originally a warehouse and associated wharf (Hay's Wharf) for the port of London, it was redevelop

activities.name: Southbank Book Market
activities.description: Second-hand book sale near the bank of the Thames. A nice place to just browse for books (classic and modern), maps and prints.

activities.name: Southbank Centre Food Market
activities.description: Dozens of market stalls selling fresh food. Each trader at this market is required to produce both ethical and sustainable products.

activities.name: Lower Marsh Street
activities.description: Lower Marsh Street has diverse food stalls, including Korean, Cuban, Nigerian, Peruvian and Ethiopian cuisines.

activities.name: Marie's Cafe
activities.description: A favourite with locals, don't be put off by the shabby exterior of this greasy spoon/Thai cafe hybrid. The Thai menu is authentic and very tasty and service is quick. Very close to the Old Vic and around 10 minutes walk

activities.name: Padella
activities.description: Daily fresh handmade pasta in a two-level restaurant right by the entrance of Borough Market. Watch the chefs prepare dishes in the open kitchen on the main floor. Listed as a Michelin Bib Gourmand (2020) since 2016. No

activities.name: Lord Nelson
activities.description: Small but unique burger restaurant and bar. Good student discounts. Good selection of burgers, and tastes great for the price. The restaurant has a lot of character, hosting memorabilia and knicknacks from the 1950s-mode

activities.name: TAS Pide
activities.description: Delightful Turkish restaurant specialising in "pide", boat-shaped flatbreads. Plenty of vegetarian options. Sample the free Turkish delights at the front desk.

activities.name: The Garrison
activities.description: Gastro-pub with fine-dining and cinema downstairs. Mains come in between £10-25.

activities.name: Ping Pong Southbank
activities.location: Festival Terrace, Southbank Centre, Belvedere Rd

activities.name: OXO Tower Restaurant Bar & Brasserie
activities.description: Offers spectacular views and the complete dining experience with mouthwatering dishes and indulgent wines to match.
activities.location: 8th Floor, Oxo Tower Wharf, Bargehouse Street, SE1 9PH

activities.name: Marble Arch
activities.description: This enormous arch was built in front of Buckingham Palace. In 1851 the expansion of the palace meant the arch could no longer remain where it was, so it was moved to this point on Hyde Park. It now stands rather sadly o
activities.location: Where the north-eastern point of Hyde Park meets the south-western tip of Marylebone

activities.name: London Central Mosque
activities.description: Islamic Cultural Centre and the main mosque in London. Visitors are welcome but must be suitably attired. The Mosque runs classes, prayers, talks and events.

activities.name: Grosvenor Square
activities.description: Nicknamed Little America, it was the long-time home of the American Embassy (which in 2018 moved to Nine Elms across the river), and a statue of President Franklin D. Roosevelt.

activities.name: St George's Hanover Square
activities.location: The Vestry, 2A Mill St, London W1S 1FX (office)

activities.name: Apsley House
activities.location: 149 Piccadilly, Hyde Park Corner, London, W1J 7NT

activities.name: Curzon Mayfair
activities.description: Retro 1970s decor, a relaxing bar area and comfortable seating combine with a great art-house billing, easily one of the best cinemas in London.

activities.name: Cordings Ltd
activities.description: Outfitter for the upmarket country set, including colourful corduroy trousers for men.

activities.name: Fortnum and Mason
activities.description: World famous as grocers to the British Royal Family, Fortnum's original store has been here in Piccadilly since 1707.

activities.name: Cad & the Dandy
activities.description: Remarkable bespoke and made-to-measure suit tailors for men. Fine English fabrics. Also offer men's wedding suit tailoring. Design your suit using their online designing tool.

activities.name: Burberry
activities.description: Visit the flagship store on Bond St to examine the famous check.

activities.name: Coco di Mama - Italian To Go - Margaret St
Maroush
activities.description: A chain of Middle Eastern food; belly dancing at night.

activities.name: Paul Marylebone
activities.description: French patisserie and bakery with some seating available.

activities.name: Neat Burger
activities.description: Vegan burgers, fries, and shakes with retro decor.

activities.name: Carluccio's
activities.description: Creative Italian cuisine, enjoy a bottle of pinot grigio at the bar while waiting for a table to free.

activities.name: Giraffe Burgers and Cocktails
activities.description: World food at great prices. Great for children

activities.name: Tapa Room
activities.description: With the more formal Providores restaurant upstairs.

activities.name: Le Gavroche
activities.description: World famous, multiple-Michelin-starred, restaurant run by Michel Roux Jr.

activities.name: National Gallery
activities.description: Houses the British national collection of western European art dating from the 13th to 19th centuries. A truly awe-inspiring collection, notable works include Hans Holbein's The Ambassadors, Van Gogh's Sunflowers and Con

activities.name: National Portrait Gallery
activities.description: The National Portrait Gallery is just around the corner from the National Gallery and is an entertaining way to learn about British history since the Tudors. Visitors walk around the gallery chronologically, viewing port

activities.name: Canada House
activities.description: The High Commission of Canada immediately opposite to Trafalgar Square maintains a regular art exhibition featuring Canadian artists.

activities.name: Leicester Square Garden
activities.description: Relax and unwind in the garden, and gaze at the activity going on all around, while listening to the buskers, street entertainers and preachers performing in the area. At the centre of the square is a fountain with a sta

activities.name: Chinatown
activities.description: The main sights to see in London's Chinatown are the expressions of Chinese culture and Eastern ambience in Chinatown, and for a good Chinese meal. There are few souvenir shops for tourists and no museums or temples.

activities.name: Chinese arches
activities.description: The three ornamental Chinese arches are worth a look.
activities.location: at each end of Gerrard St and at the entrance to Macclesfield St

activities.name: Chinatown phone booths
activities.description: Chinatown does incorporate some entertaining combinations of British and Chinese culture, such as the phone booths with pagoda-style sloping roofs.

activities.name: Curzon Soho Cinema
activities.description: Voted "London's Number 1 Cinema" by Time Out readers, great bar and a fantastic art-house program.

activities.name: Prince Charles Cinema
activities.description: The cheapest and in some ways most interesting cinema in the area. They screen the latest films, and also have an interesting selection of foreign and art house films. They often have theme nights. Get hold of the progra

activities.name: The Hippodrome Casino
activities.description: A former theatre, now home to a large casino with live cabaret shows and a steakhouse restaurant. Also sells souvenirs, poker chips and plaque keyrings that can be purchased from the cashier. If you stay here long have a

activities.name: Wong Kei
activities.description: A Chinatown institution. Popular with Londoners and visitors alike and possibly the best value Chinese restaurant in the whole of London. Spread across four floors, this restaurant is infamous for its surly, abrupt servi

activities.name: The Moon Under Water - JD Wetherspoon
The Portrait Restaurant
activities.description: Offers spectacular food accompanied by spectacular art on the 5th floor of the National Portrait Gallery. A must-do dining experience.

activities.name: Tokyo Diner
activities.description: Offers excellent and well-priced Japanese food.

activities.name: Vantra Vegan
activities.description: Friendly, quiet vegan restaurant near the edge of Chinatown. Try the mock meats.

activities.name: Leong's Legend
activities.description: Serves Taiwanese fare. Chilli crab and omelette dishes are specialities.

activities.name: C&R Cafe Restaurant
activities.description: Worth checking out for cheap Malaysian-Singaporean eats.

activities.name: Golden Gate Cake Shop
activities.description: Place Chinese cakes on a tray and take them to the cashier.

activities.name: St Giles-without-Cripplegate
activities.description: Medieval Grade I listed church. This church played a key role in the English Revolution and was the parish church of some of the most decisive Puritans: Oliver Cromwell was married and this is where John Milton was burie

activities.name: St Sepulchre-without-Newgate
activities.description: Grade I listed Saxon church founded before the 12th century. The exterior was mostly constructed in the 15th century, and the interior in the 19th. The north side of the church houses a musician's chapel opened in 1955.

activities.name: Old Bailey
activities.location: between Holborn Circus and St Paul's Cathedral, tube: {{station

activities.name: St Bartholomew-the-Great
activities.description: Founded in 1123, by jester-turned-monk Rahere, this Norman church is one of the oldest in London. It was damaged in the Dissolution but managed to escape both the Great Fire and the Blitz. This is a Grade I listed buildi

activities.name: St Botolph's Aldersgate
activities.description: A medieval church that was rebuilt in the late 18th century, noted for its well-preserved interior. The former churchyard was converted into a public space in 1880, called Postman's Park as it was frequented by workers f

activities.name: Museum of London
activities.description: Now permanently closed, will be replaced by new museum at the former Smithfield market site in 2026. The Museum of London Docklands remains open.
activities.location: London Wall (NB: this is a street!)

activities.name: Guildhall Art Gallery and Roman Amphitheatre
Dr Johnson's House
activities.description: Dr Samuel Johnson was the highly distinguished 18th-century "man of letters", best known for his comprehensive English Dictionary of 1755, but also for his prolific output of poems, essays and novels. Something of a "hid

activities.name: London Mithraeum
activities.description: The London Mithraeum is a 3rd century Roman mithraeum that was discovered in Walbrook during construction work in 1954. The entire site was relocated several times before being returned to its original site as part of th

activities.name: Barts Pathology Museum
activities.description: Quirky medical museum. Only open to the public for scheduled evening events.

activities.name: Barbican Conservatory
activities.description: The second biggest greenhouse in London, containing over 2,000 species of tropical plants as well as birds and fish.

activities.name: Blitz Plaque
activities.description: The first of tens of thousands of bombs to hit London in World War II fell here in 1940.

activities.name: Baltic Exchange
activities.description: The world's main marketplace for ship broking.

activities.name: Noble St wall fragment
activities.description: Remains of the wall that surrounded the City of London for almost two thousand years. The parts around the Barbican are mostly Tudor due to maintenance (Roman remains can be seen in and around the Tower of London). Other

activities.name: Lloyds of London
activities.description: The headquarters of world's most famous insurance market, housed in a revolutionary (at the time) bizarre, Matrix-like glass-and-steel building designed by Richard Rogers, with all support services (lifts, ventilation, e

activities.name: London Stock Exchange
activities.description: After leaving its brutalist skyscraper on Old Broad St, the London Stock Exchange now resides on Paternoster Sq. Dating back to 1698, it is one of the world's oldest and largest stock markets.

activities.name: London Bullion Market Association
activities.description: This is where the world gold price is set twice a day.

activities.name: London Metal Exchange
activities.description: The LME is the leading centre for non-ferrous metals trading. It is also the last financial market in London which still retains open outcry trading.

activities.name: The Gherkin
activities.description: Designed by one of Britain's leading architects, Sir Norman Foster, this curved glass skyscraper was the 2004 recipient of the Stirling Architectural Prize for Best Building. It was built on the site of the Baltic Exchan

activities.name: Willis Building
activities.description: Completed in 2008 opposite Lloyd's of London.

activities.name: Leadenhall Building
activities.description: Another Richard Rogers creation, nicknamed the Cheesegrater. Completed in 2014. Also opposite Lloyd's.

activities.name: 20 Fenchurch Street
activities.description: The unusual "walkie scorchie" profile of this skyscraper by Rafael Vinoly has seen it grab the headlines, as did the "death rays" caused by the sun's heat reflecting off the building onto the street below when it was fir

activities.name: Horizon 22
activities.description: London's highest viewing platform that is free to the public. Sitting at the 58th floor of an office building, and the tallest building in City of London proper, it gives visitors a 300-degree view of London.

activities.name: Barbican Architecture Tour
activities.description: 90-minute tour of the beautifully ugly brutalist site.
activities.location: starts at the Advance Ticket desk, Silk St entrance

activities.name: Digital Revolution
activities.description: An exhibition of digital art and creativity, including film, music and games, and the effect of technology on the arts. Runs until 14 September.

activities.name: The Fashion World of Jean Paul Gaultier
activities.description: An exhibition devoted to the French couturier featuring over a hundred garments and costumes. Runs until 25 August.

activities.name: Lord Mayor's Show
activities.description: The ceremony celebrates the appointment of the new Lord Mayor of the City of London. It is one of the great annual processions held in all London.

activities.name: London Walks
activities.description: Consider going along on one of the many excellent guided tours of the City, often with an evocative theme for example ghosts or Jack the Ripper.

activities.name: London Night Tour
activities.location: Departing from Coach stop 3, Bulleid Way

activities.name: Tesco Express
activities.description: Small, local branch of the supermarket

activities.name: Sainsbury's Local
activities.description: Small, local branch of the supermarket. In a pedestrian square near St. Paul's Cathedral.

activities.name: HOP Vietnamese
activities.description: Local chain with several branches in the City.

activities.name: Eataly London
activities.description: An Italian food hall and eatery opened in 2021 along the lines of the Turin original. It's east side of Liverpool St station.

activities.name: Comptoir Gascon
activities.description: Temporarily closed as of May 2022. French restaurant and delicatessen.

activities.name: Smiths of Smithfield
activities.description: Grade II listed four-floor restaurant in Smithfield Market.

activities.name: Wood Street Bar and Restaurant
activities.description: Real ale, nice food and a relaxed atmosphere.

activities.name: Duck and Waffle
activities.description: All day dining on the 40th floor of the Heron Tower. Popular in the very late night for a bite after going out and waiting to watch the sunrise over the rest of London.

activities.name: Angler
activities.description: One Michelin-starred British seafood restaurant with views over the City.

activities.name: Camera Museum
activities.description: A downstairs museum dedicated to cameras from the 1800s to present day, including a camera rifle like the one used in Licence to Kill. The museum is a quick visit and worth the stop even for someone with a moderate inter

activities.name: Brunei Gallery, SOAS University of London
activities.description: An exhibition space that hosts a programme of changing contemporary and historical exhibitions from Asia, Africa and the Middle East, aiming to present and promote cultures from these regions and to be a student resource

activities.name: British Telecom Tower
activities.description: 191-m (627-ft) skyscraper can be seen from far away and is the sixth highest building in UK.

activities.name: Birkbeck Institute for the Moving Image
activities.description: Cinema owned by Birkbeck, University of London, featuring screenings and lectures. Most events are free but pre-booking is advised.
activities.location: 43 Gordon Sq, Birkbeck, University of London, WC1H 0PD

activities.name: London Mathematical Society
activities.description: Based on Russell Square, they give lectures and exhibitions, for those numerically inclined.
activities.location: De Morgan House, 57-58 Russell Square, WC1B 4HS

activities.name: Walks
activities.description: Bloomsbury has a number of famous walks that cover the lives and works of the Bloomsbury Group.

activities.name: Bloomsbury Festival
activities.description: Takes place in the Summer around the environs of Bloomsbury, concentrated in Russell Square, and hosts arts and crafts fair, children's painting classes and dance workshops. The food stalls are good value too.

activities.name: Roberto Revilla
activities.description: Bespoke tailoring services and luxury accessories.
activities.location: Savva Tailors Workroom, Shropshire House, 179 Tottenham Court Rd, W1T 7NZ

activities.name: Tottenham Court Road
activities.description: The centre for electronics retailers in London. If you are visiting the city and need to buy or replace an electronic item, you will not find a better selection nor better prices than here. Explore both sides of the stre

activities.name: The Brunswick Centre
activities.description: Shopping centre with a large Waitrose supermarket and some other shops.
activities.location: between Brunswick Square and Russell Square

activities.name: Dal Fiorentino Fitzrovia
activities.description: A small sandwich shop away from the hustle and bustle of Fitzrovia that serves a quick lunch for under £10. Has meal deals! (updated Nov 2025)

activities.name: Indian YMCA
activities.description: The hostel's restaurant is open to the public.

activities.name: Great Court Restaurant
activities.location: The British Museum, Great Russell St, WC1B 3DG

activities.name: Master Wei Xi'an
activities.description: Northern Chinese restaurant, specializing in authentic Xi'an dishes. Try the biangbiang noodles or the steamed dumplings.

activities.name: Memorial Scrolls Trust Museum
activities.location: 3rd floor, Kent House, Rutland Gardens, SW7 1BX

activities.name: Chelsea Physic Garden
activities.description: Garden founded by apothecaries in the 17th century to the medicinal properties of plants. It was only opened to the public in the 1980s, when it became a charity. The heat-sink caused by its thick walls, combined with th

activities.name: Diana Memorial Fountain
activities.description: More of an artificial stream than a fountain, flowing in an oval through an open grassy area which, in normal summers, is a popular paddling spot for children and grown-ups alike.
activities.location: south of the Serpentine, by the bridge

activities.name: Rose Garden
activities.description: The roses are mixed with herbaceous plants and bedding flowers so there's always some horticultural interest no matter the time of year, though the roses' peak is in June. Two interesting fountains depict Diana the huntr

activities.name: Holocaust Memorial
activities.description: A quiet birch wood surrounds an understated memorial stone inscribed with a quotation from the Book of Lamentation: "For these I weep. Streams of tears flow from my eyes because of the destruction of my people.".
activities.location: between the rose garden and the Serpentine

activities.name: The Serpentine
activities.description: The Serpentine is a long, thin artificial lake within Hyde Park which contains a range of waterfowl and fish. It was first made by damming the River Westbourne but it is now fed by three boreholes. The western end of the

activities.name: Speakers Corner
activities.description: By custom, in this far northeastern corner of the park (by the Marble Arch Tube stop near [[London/Mayfair-Marylebone

activities.name: 7 July Memorial
activities.description: A memorial made out of 52 stainless steel columns representing each of the 52 victims who died in a series of terrorist attacks in the London bombings on July 7th, 2005.

activities.name: Albert Memorial
activities.description: Highly detailed, Gothic-style monument to commemorate Prince Albert, husband of Queen Victoria. Opposite the Royal Albert Hall.

activities.name: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: Diana, Princess of Wales Memorial Playground

activities.name: Elfin Oak
activities.description: A 900-year-old tree stump carved and painted to feature elves and small animals.

activities.name: The Round Pond
activities.description: Full of swans, geese, gulls, and other birds. Deckchairs can be hired for £1.50. The park benches and grassy areas are free.
activities.location: half way along the Broad Walk

activities.name: The Serpentine Gallery
activities.description: A nice modern art gallery, near to the Serpentine. Too small to host a permanent collection, instead hosts temporary exhibitions which can last anything from a few weeks to over a year. Each summer a pavilion next to the

activities.name: Roper's Garden
activities.description: Small green area, with benches, by the Thames. Created in the crater of a World War II bomb site, it contains an ancient cherry tree to commemorate the visit of Gunji Koizumi, the man who introduced judo to the country.

activities.name: Billionaires' Row
activities.description: One of the most expensive residential streets on the planet! Most of the buildings here are embassies or ambassadorial residences but the rest of the mansions belong to some of the richest people in the country (or elsew
activities.location: tube: High Street Kensington or Notting Hill Gate

activities.name: Brompton Oratory
activities.description: Beautiful Italianate church created during the Catholic revival of the 19th century.

activities.name: Carlyle's House
activities.description: Now preserved by the National Trust, this 18th-century house was the home of the historian Thomas Carlyle from 1834 and now houses a museum dedicated to his life and work.

activities.name: Chelsea Old Church
activities.description: This church on the bank of the Thames was built in 1157. It is the only church in London with chained books. A statue of Thomas More sits in front of the church.

activities.name: Crosby Hall
activities.description: All that remains of a Tudor mansion built in the [[London/City of London

activities.name: Holy Trinity Church
activities.description: From their website: "In common with the aims of the Arts & Crafts Movement, the architect of Holy Trinity Church, John Dando Sedding, believed that a church should be 'wrought and painted over with everything that has li

activities.name: Royal Hospital
activities.description: A retirement home for soldiers created by King Charles II. Tours around the listed building and grounds are regular and include the museum (which can be visited separately) whose exhibits contain military memorabilia don

activities.name: Brompton Cemetery
activities.location: entrances on Old Brompton Road and Fulham Rd

activities.name: Penfold Post Box
activities.description: One of the few Victorian hexagonal Penfold post boxes still in use. Although there are others in this district, this one in notable for a narrow escape during World War II: minor bomb damage can still be seen in the base

activities.name: Police Box
activities.location: Earls Court Road, outside Earl's Court station

activities.name: Royal Geographical Society
activities.description: The UK learned society of geographers, founded in 1830. Hosts weekly lectures and other gatherings, many of which are free to attend to any member of the public. The RGS has funded many British expeditions over the years

activities.name: Saatchi Gallery
activities.location: Duke Of York's HQ, King's Road, SW3 4RY

activities.name: London Cru
activities.description: Urban winery based in West London run by a team of experts that include international winemaker Gavin Monery and Master of Wine student Mark Andrew. The winery offers public tours, wine tastings, courses, and custom wine

activities.name: Royal Albert Hall
activities.description: Since opening in 1871 as part of Queen Victoria's memorial to her late husband it has become one of the most famous venues in the UK, and remains one of London's main concert halls. It still mainly caters for a classical

activities.name: Royal Court Theatre
activities.description: Britain's leading national theatre company dedicated to new work by innovative writers from the UK and around the world. Productions that have transferred to great acclaim include Enron by Lucy Prebble and Jerusalem by J

activities.name: The Troubadour
activities.description: Famous bohemian cafe with a pleasing menu of hearty dishes such as fishcakes, burgers and delicious filling salads. Good wine list too and lovely leafy garden out the back. There is a great little music venue downstairs

activities.name: The Ronson Theatre
activities.description: IMAX cinema screen at the Science Museum showing documentary films and blockbusters.

activities.name: Institut Français du Royaume-Uni
activities.description: The first in a worldwide network of institutes to promote and present the best of French culture. The site includes a café-restaurant, a cinema showing French films, the largest French library in the UK and a French lang

activities.name: Ciné Lumière
activities.description: Watch French-language movies in the Institute's cinema.

activities.name: Language Centre
activities.description: Learn French or take courses on French culture from France's official teaching centre in the UK. Intensive courses can be completed in just two weeks, general courses are closer to 10–15 weeks.

activities.name: La Médiathèque
activities.description: The largest French library in the UK. Read and borrow French books from the library. Non-members are free to browse; members can borrow books.

activities.name: Boating on the Serpentine
activities.description: Rowing boats and pedalos available for hire. Alternatively, you can go on a (very short) 'cruise' on the solar shuttle, a silent boat powered entirely by the rays of the mighty London sun.

activities.name: Swimming in the Serpentine
activities.description: Famous swimming club in Hyde Park's lake. The winter months are members-only (hardy club members traditionally take part in a swim on Christmas Day), but during the summer months the lido is open to the general public. A

activities.name: Hyde Park Tennis and Sports Centre
activities.description: Contains six hard tennis courts, two mini tennis courts, a six-rink lawn bowling green and a nine-hole putting green; plus support facilities such as changing rooms and a café. Bowling greens, tennis courts and nearby fo

activities.name: The Duke of York Square Shopping Complex
activities.description: Has a range of spacious branches of popular fashion chains, but lacks some of the road's character, however the small adjoining public space of Duke of York square is a welcome place for a rest between shopping, and a po
activities.location: just off King's Road, near Sloane Square

activities.name: Harrods
activities.description: The most famous store in London, favoured by the British establishment and Arab royalty and owned by the state of Qatar. As of 2023, their [https://www.harrods.com/en-us/faqs/visiting-our-store website FAQ] says about dr

activities.name: Harvey Nichols
activities.description: Large department store full of designer goods and an excellent cafe.

activities.name: The Hummingbird Bakery
activities.description: A nice little bakery with wonderful cupcakes. Try their red velvet cupcake that is incredibly popular with the locals; it even comes in a gluten-free version.

activities.name: H.R. Owen Ferrari London
activities.description: If the expensive clothes and products in other shops don't appeal, expensive cars are also available in this area.

activities.name: King's Road
activities.description: One of London's smartest fashion streets, having evolved from the cutting-edge of bohemia and innovative fashion in the 1960s to a more genteel place to indulge in retail therapy, albeit with a notable presence of trendy
activities.location: tube: Sloane Square, for north-east end

activities.name: Lamborghini London
activities.description: If you fancy a new car and have a lot of spare change, it might be worth a look. Poorer people are allowed to look at the shiny cars too.

activities.name: Sloane Street
activities.description: Lined with high-end designer label stores, such as Chanel, Dior, Fendi, Gucci, Hermès, Jimmy Choo, Louis Vitton, and many more.

activities.name: Whole Foods Market
activities.description: The Kensington branch of this American chain of natural food supermarkets is the largest in the world.

activities.name: The Carnival Store
activities.description: Fancy dress stocking an extensive range of budget to mid-range costumes, masks and acessories, staff are very approachable and prepared to answer queries.

activities.name: La Delizia 1986
activities.description: Quaint Italian bistro offers 18 different pizzas, three risottos, various pasta dishes, gnocchi and other traditional Italian entrees. It’s top-notch food at a great value.

activities.name: Comptoir Libanais South Kensington
activities.description: Delicious Lebanese food, served without fuss, and fairly promptly considering the crowds. Wide range of vegetarian choices. The pretty, colourful dining room includes several long tables, where you will be seated next to

activities.name: Côte Brasserie
activities.description: Modern restaurant/bar at the Sloane Square Hotel serving French cuisine. Popular lunch place for people working in the area.

activities.name: Da Mario
activities.description: Popular family-run ristorante with great pizzas and Italian dishes. Come early or get on the waiting list or book in advance.

activities.name: Café and Terrace at Harvey Nichols
activities.description: The aptly name café on the fifth floor of Harvey Nichols. A roof terrace gives a view of the rooftops of Knightsbridge.

activities.name: Great India Tandoori
activities.description: One of Chelsea's most popular restaurants. Established by Satir Ahmed in 1960.

activities.name: Kensington Creperie
activities.description: A small, cute, often crowded cafe and authentic French creperie, popular with the local French and various other South Ken expats. Given its location near the tube, it is a fine place to sit outside and watch the fashion

activities.name: Gordon Ramsay
activities.description: The original, flagship branch of the Ramsay empire. You are highly unlikely to find the man himself behind the stove these days, but this triple Michelin-starred eatery deserves its reputation as one of the finest on the

activities.name: Charing Cross and Charing Cross Station
activities.description: Old train station with an enormous office and shopping complex in glass and pale stone. A lavish hotel is also on the site. The name comes from the Queen Eleanor (13th century) gothic pillar outside the station, which is

activities.name: Covent Garden Piazza and Central Market
St Martins in the Fields
activities.description: A neoclassical church that stands opposite the National Gallery. Since World War I, the homeless have sought shelter at this church, a tradition that continues to this day.

activities.name: Edith Cavell Memorial
activities.description: Statue in honour of the World War I nursing heroine.

activities.name: Aldwych station
activities.description: The TfL-backed Hidden London offers tours around the disused Aldwych tube station which had its own branch on the Piccadilly line. The tour explains the station's unusual history including its use for protection (for bot

activities.name: St Martin's Theatre
activities.description: The theatre which has been showing Agatha Christie's The Mousetrap continuously since 1952!

activities.name: English National Opera
activities.location: London Coliseum, St. Martin's Lane, WC2N 4ES

activities.name: The Garden Cinema
activities.description: Independent cinema specialising in cult classics and arthouse films

activities.name: The Astrology Shop
activities.description: The place to buy astrology books and tarot cards.

activities.name: Ben's Cookies
activities.description: Very nice cookies - try their white chocolate with macadamia cookies. Price is about £1 per cookie, and the cookies here are almost twice as big as those at Millie's. They also have branches at 12 Kensington Arcade, Kens

activities.name: Monmouth Coffee
activities.description: Some people say this is the best coffee in London. Primarily a venue for quality coffee roasters, the shop on Monmouth street has a tasting room, i.e. a café, where you can marvel at the fact that not everywhere in Londo

activities.name: Thomas Neals Centre
activities.description: A nice shopping plaza with clothes shops, design shops and cafes.

activities.name: The Tintin Shop
activities.description: The place to go in Britain for fans of the Belgian reporter. Sells non-fiction books, clothes, posters, figurines and memorabilia, as well as each of Hergé's 24 Adventures of Tintin comic books in English, the original F

activities.name: Kryolan City London
activities.description: This is where the professionals buy make-up, so on the pricey side, but the range of products kept is large. Staff are highly approachable even on specialist queries.

activities.name: Forbidden Planet
activities.description: Massive "geek supplies" store for London and the South-East. Merchandise (T-shirts, toys, figurines, etc.) on the ground floor, comics and manga (all genres) and SF, fantasy and horror books in the basement. Lots of U.S.

activities.name: David Drummond at Pleasures of Past Times
Quinto Bookshop & Francis Edwards
activities.description: Huge collection. Francis Edwards have been in business here since 1856.

activities.name: Arthur Beale
activities.description: A quite unusual shop for the centre of London. A marine supply store which sells numerous items related to yachting: from clothing to sailing hardware and accessories. The shop has been at its current location for over 1

activities.name: Brown's
activities.description: Always buzzing, this popular restaurant is frequented by hip (often single) clientele, who are served by attentive staff. Meals are good, the braised lamb shank is especially tasty.

activities.name: Maison Malinowski Brasserie
activities.description: Serves really delicious crêpes among other things. Perfect for a break while shopping!

activities.name: Cafe Pacifico
activities.description: London's original Mexican Cantina, serving all your typical Mexican food along with a good range of margaritas and tequila.

activities.name: La Perla
activities.description: Another of Cafe Pacifico's venues, this one is slightly smaller with more of a bar feeling.

activities.name: Scoop
activities.description: This place is popular among the university students in London. Come here for some genuine Italian gelato or coffee and cake. Try their hazelnut gelato, which is simply amazing.

activities.name: Rules
activities.description: Claims to the oldest restaurant still-going in London dating back to 1798. It's high-class decor matches its posh diners. Rules has a dress code: "jacket or tie not required, but smart casual preferred. No shorts."

activities.name: Jack The Ripper Museum
activities.location: 12 Cable St, Tower Hill, Whitechapel, E1 8JG

activities.name: Regents Canal
activities.description: Walk up the Regent's Canal to Islington via Haggerston.

activities.name: Green Bridge
activities.description: Walk over the Green Bridge, a unique structure that joins Mile End Park together with its urban nature reserve, taking the park over the busy A11 road, ensuring uninterrupted greenery for several miles, from Limehouse to

activities.name: Cycle
activities.description: Hire a bike and cycle around the island. There are lots of cycle paths and great riverside views of the [[London/City of London

activities.name: Jack the Ripper Tour
activities.description: For those of you with a morbid fascination with this 19th-century murderer, this will hopefully feed your curiosity. Approx 2 hours. Book through website.

activities.name: Shoreditch Street Art Tour
activities.description: A tour of the constantly changing street art around this part of the East End. Saturday times may vary.

activities.name: Petticoat Lane Market
activities.description: Traditionally the East End market where nearly anything could be found (legal or not) now mostly cheaper mainstream clothes. Main market Sunday, smaller market M-F in Wentworth St.

activities.name: Brick Lane
activities.description: The Whitechapel end of Brick Lane is filled with South Asian (mainly Bangladeshi) shops and restaurants, selling music, spices, tasty sweets and sari material.

activities.name: Brick Lane Market
activities.description: Selling second hand clothes, bric-a-brac, books, household goods and electronics.
activities.location: overground: Shoreditch High St, running from Shoreditch High St Overground Stn to Bethnal Green Rd and spreading out into Sclater and Cheshire Sts

activities.name: Tatty Devine
activities.description: Little shop specialising in accessories for men and women, such as volume level brooches and newspaper print ties.

activities.name: Rough Trade East
activities.description: Boasts a large vinyl selection, books, merchandise, and hosts live events 7 days a week.

activities.name: The Limehouse
activities.description: Lunchtime menus include a mixture of continental and international meals. A snack menu is available in the evening. Two mezzanine areas at the bar for private party bookings.

activities.name: Gaucho Grill
activities.description: The Argentinian cowboy, the gaucho, made famous the tradition of cooking fresh meat over charcoal grills. The large horseshoe bar offers traditional South American cocktails and beer.

activities.name: Plateau Restaurant Bar and Grill
activities.description: Modern French restaurant, part of the Conran restaurants group. More informal Bar and Grill includes steak grills and hearty regional French dishes. Two bars.

activities.name: Quadrato
activities.description: International classics for breakfast and northern Italian cuisine for lunch and dinner. The Tuscan Sunday Brunch and cocktails at bar Quadrato should not be missed. Watching chef Sebastiano Spriveri at work is a main att

activities.name: Royal China
activities.description: Popular restaurant with a great view of the Thames River. Decorated with Royal China gives a contemporary feel. Enjoy dim-sum and an extensive menu.

activities.name: The Greedy Cow
activities.description: A cosy restaurant serving burgers with unusual and exotic ingredients, along with more traditional fare such as chicken or beef. Salads and steaks also available.

activities.name: Orange Room
activities.description: A good place for chilled-out lunches.

activities.name: Bengal Village
activities.description: Good food, good value, pleasant atmosphere and good service.

activities.name: Brick Lane Beigel Bake
activities.description: A local institution, Beigel Bake is more celebrated of the two and rated by some as the freshest most delicious beigel you will ever have. Their salt beef is more highly rated than Beigel Shop and a salt beef beigel with

activities.name: Britain's First & Best Beigel Shop
activities.description: While less celebrated than its near neighbour, Beigel Shop is still well worth a visit. While Beigel Bake is a local institution, it is reflected in its limited range. As a result, Beigel Shop works harder for your cash,

activities.name: New Tayyabs
activities.description: Long established and arguably the best South Asian restaurant in the Whitechapel area. Serves traditional food in a pleasant if slightly frenetic atmosphere. Good value and fast efficient service. Particularly recommende

activities.name: Lahore Kebabhouse
activities.description: Good value, extremely friendly staff and is able to cater to big groups (up to 20 people). Plus, it has a BYOB licence, keeping costs down.

activities.name: Upmarket
activities.description: Indoor weekend market with lots of stalls serving cuisine from all over the world, from Tibetan dumplings to Venezuelan arepas. Has a lively street food atmosphere and an excellent array of vegan options.

activities.name: Keu Deli
activities.description: The banh mi sandwich is a crusty baguette filled with terrines, meats, lightly pickled vegetables, fragrant herbs and almost always mayonnaise.

activities.name: Mala
activities.description: Expensive Indian cuisine. Tandooris, Biryanis, and other specialties. View of the docks, full bar.

activities.name: Laxeiro
activities.description: Family-run Spanish restaurant established in 1982. Amazing Spanish food. Great for lunch when at the flower market.

activities.name: Boxpark
activities.description: A trendy mall built out of shipping containers, full of hipsters and groups of twentysomethings at the weekend.

activities.name: The Bike Shed Motorcycle Club
activities.description: Restaurant where motorcycles ride past you at your table. You can ride in and park your motorbike near the tables, or come without a bike just for the atmosphere.

activities.name: Xi'an Biang Biang Noodles
activities.description: Authentic northern Chinese cuisine. The specialty is a classic Shaanxi dish, the thick biang biang noodles in the restaurant's name.

activities.name: Ozone Coffee Roasters
activities.description: Delicious brunch spot and coffee shop. No reservations on weekends, walk-ins only. Well-behaved dogs welcome.

activities.name: The Cutty Sark
activities.description: A preserved tea and wool clipper built in 1869 which set the record for passage from Australia under sail. The ship was damaged by fire on 21 May 2007, but much of the ship's structure and rigging had already been remove

activities.name: The Fan Museum
activities.description: The world's largest fan museum, for those who are big fans of fans. Closed in winter.

activities.name: Greenwich Park
activities.description: Situated on a hill rising up from Greenwich town centre, the park offers impressive views from the hilltop across the River Thames to Docklands and the City of London. The park also provides a setting for several histori

activities.name: The Royal Observatory
activities.description: The home of Greenwich Mean Time and the Prime Meridian line, this is one of the most important historic scientific sites in the world. It was founded by Charles II in 1675 and is, by international decree, the official st

activities.name: The National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Museum devoted to the maritime role in Britain's history. Contains the UK's national collection of Maritime artefacts (although do not expect much in the way of whole ships). One of the buildings housing the museum is th

activities.name: The Royal Naval College
activities.description: Built by Christopher Wren in 1694 as the Greenwich Hospital for the relief and support of seamen and their dependents and for the improvement of navigation. It became the Royal Naval College in 1869. In 1999 the Universi
activities.location: Old Royal Naval College, 2 Cutty Sark Gdns, SE10 9NN

activities.name: Ranger's House
activities.description: At Blackheath, the Ranger's House maintains a large ceramic collection.

activities.name: Greenwich Peninsula Ecology Park
activities.description: A hidden-away green space and wildlife haven in the middle of urban Greenwich. Comes alive in the summer with brightly coloured insects and birds.
activities.location: The Ecology Park Gatehouse, Thames Path, John Harrison Way, SE10 0QZ

activities.name: Eltham Palace
activities.description: One of the most notable art deco buildings in London which was built and owned by the Courtaulds family of textile fame. Administered by English Heritage.

activities.name: Charlton House
activities.description: Completed in 1612, the finest and best preserved Jacobean mansion in London.

activities.name: Severndroog Castle
activities.description: An 18th-century folly built as a memorial to Sir William James by his widow. Today you can climb the 86 steps to the top of the castle for amazing views over London and seven different counties.

activities.name: Greenwich Heritage and Meridian Tours
activities.description: Operated by the Greenwich Tour Guides Association, the original guiding organisation that has operated in Greenwich for 25 years. Walking tours take place twice a day (every day, excluding 24-26 Dec, 1 Jan and London Mar

activities.name: Greenwich Royal Tours
activities.description: Runs a range of more in-depth tours including Greenwich Day Tour, Greenwich Highlights Half Day Tour, Greenwich Food Tour and National Maritime Museum Tour. Tickets must be booked in advance and tours leave from outside
activities.location: meets outside the Greenwich Visitors Centre

activities.name: Greenwich Bus Tours
activities.description: A range of different bus tours, including to some destinations outside of Greenwich town centre which are harder to reach.

activities.name: Meantime Brewery Tours
activities.description: Since being founded in Greenwich in 2000, beers from the award-winning Meantime Brewery have spread across the country. Still produced in Greenwich, tours of the site are available and include a tasting session. A bar an
activities.location: Unit 4-5 Lawrence Trading Estate, Blackwall Lane SE10 0AR

activities.name: Trinity Laban Conservatoire of Music and Dance
activities.description: An internationally renowned school for music and dance training based in the Old Royal Naval College and a contemporary site by Deptford Creek. Put on musical and dance performances across Greenwich throughout the year -

activities.name: Odeon
activities.description: The large Odeon multiplex has 18-screens, but is a bit out-of-the way, at Bugsby Way, on the way to the O2 Arena.

activities.name: Up The Creek Comedy Club
activities.description: Comedy club. Hosts hilarious stand-up as well as music nights.

activities.name: Blackheath Heath Fireworks Display
activities.description: Every Guy Fawkes night (5th of November) Lewisham council put on a spectacular free fireworks display. In the past few years crowds of over 100,000 have amassed, so arrive early to secure a good spot. The heath also host

activities.name: Greenwich Tall Ships Festival
activities.description: Annual festival held at the start of July. Tall sailing ships cruise down the Thames from Woolwich, with Greenwich town centre being a fantasic place to view them. Each evening of the festival concludes with a spectacula

activities.name: London Marathon
activities.description: The London Marathon takes place annually, usually on the last Sunday of April. For both wheelchair racers and runners (elite, club and fun - many in fancy dress), it starts from the south side of Greenwich Park, on the c

activities.name: Charlton Athletic FC
activities.description: They play soccer in the Championship, England's second tier. The women's team play in the Championship, their second tier, at Bexleyheath in Dartford.

activities.name: NG1 Golf
activities.description: Urban driving range against the spectacular backdrop of Canary Wharf. Restaurant and bar on site. 18 years + age policy after 6PM.

activities.name: Bunker 51 laser tag and paintball
activities.description: Let off some steam for a couple of hours with laser tag or paintball at an underground Cold War themed bunker in part of industrial Greenwich. Great for kids (or just your inner-kid!). Next to the Thames Barrier - which

activities.name: Walk the Thames Path
activities.description: Greenwich is home to the last 4 miles (6.4 km) of the 184-mile-long Thames Path, organised by National Trails. From Cutty Sark, the path follows the river up and around the Greenwich Peninsula before ending at the Thames
activities.location: between Cutty Sark and the Thames Barrier

activities.name: Greenwich Vintage Market
activities.description: The name is a bit deceptive but it does have plenty of old books, music, clothes and jewellery.

activities.name: The Central Market
activities.description: The largest part of the market that sells homeware, furniture and books.

activities.name: The Kings Arms
activities.description: This pub is well situated for the tourist attractions in Greenwich and does good bar food.

activities.name: The Mogul Tandoori
activities.description: This restaurant provides excellent Nepalese and Northern Indian cuisine in an unusual and interesting setting (as long as you get seated downstairs, below street level).

activities.name: Tai Won Mein noodle houses
activities.description: Offers budget Chinese cuisine, at a budget price if you're after a bargain rather than quality. They only take cash. Noodle Time and Saigon are located around the street, facing each other, and offer similar fare/price.

activities.name: Old Brewery
activities.description: Part of the local Meantime Brewery's growing empire with a massive enclosed outdoor area - great in the summer months. Part of the same building as the tourist information centre, and perhaps more popular with tourists t

activities.name: Greenwich Market Food Court
activities.description: Expanded in 2016, Greenwich Market's outdoor food court is now a dedicated section of the market. Dozens of street food stalls offer a range of cuisines from around the world. Seating is limited, so it is best to buy you

activities.name: Buenos Aires Cafe
activities.description: Casual Argentinian cafe with steaks, Spanish-themed food and brunch. Relaxing orangery and garden at the rear.

activities.name: Rivington Greenwich
activities.description: Modern British food with seasonal ingredients. Next door to the Greenwich Picture House.

activities.name: Goddards at Greenwich
activities.description: Open since 1890, London's most famous pie and mash restaurant. Enjoy the capital's traditional meal - handmade fresh every day. Footballer David Beckham is known to be a fan and makes occasional visits.

activities.name: Zaibatsu
activities.description: Japanese fusion restaurant. Food more impressive than the basic decor. You can bring your own alcohol.

activities.name: The Golden Chippy
activities.description: Fish and chip shop. Traditional no-frills 'chippy' - takeaway or eat in.

activities.name: Street Feast - Public (Woolwich)
activities.description: A covered market from the 1930s has been transformed into an epic indoor space, with rainbow roof, pool tables and massive seating area. Home to eight of street-food traders, five bars and South-East London vibes.

activities.name: Blackheath Tea Hut
activities.description: A hut that has been serving tea to travellers on the A2 since 1924.

activities.name: The Ivy Cafe Blackheath
activities.description: British classics - great for brunch, afternoon tea or dinner. Reserve in advance if you're planning to be in the area!

activities.name: Waterlow Park
activities.description: A secret jewel of a park. Beautifully landscaped grounds, hills, mature trees, lakes, a walled garden, visitor's centre, café, tennis courts, and children's playgrounds. Bequeathed to the public as a "garden for the gard

activities.name: The Bathing Ponds
activities.description: There are three notable outdoor bathing ponds on the heath. The Men's Pond and Women's Pond are both part of the line of small lakes on the eastern edge of the heath known as Highgate Ponds, and the Mixed Pond is part of

activities.name: Parliament Hill
activities.description: This is a high part of the Heath with clear open vistas, and the views looking south over the city are quite wonderful. Highly recommended on a clear morning, when you can see from Canary Wharf in the east all the way to
activities.location: southern side of the Heath between Hampstead and Highgate Ponds

activities.name: Farmer's Market
activities.description: A regular farmer's market selling fresh organic produce every Saturday

activities.name: Hampstead Antiques Emporium
activities.description: More than 30 antique dealers permanently occupy this small arcade and adjacent courtyard. Several genres represented including furniture, paintings, toys and textiles.

activities.name: The Village Haberdashery
activities.location: Hardy Building, West Hampstead Square, 6, Heritage Ln, NW6 2BR

activities.name: Parliament Hill Café
activities.description: The only cafe actually on Hampstead Heath, and a nice place to stop for a drink or quick bite after exploring the heath.
activities.location: off Highgate Rd between the tennis courts/bowling green and the bandstand

activities.name: Bohemia House
activities.location: 74 West End Ln, West Hampstead, NW6 2LX

cultural.tip: Queue properly. Cutting in line is a serious social faux pas.
Stand on the right side of escalators on the Tube. Walk on the left.
Tipping 10-15% is standard at sit-down restaurants. Check if service is included.
The British love understatement. Avoid being too loud in public.
Get an Oyster card or use contactless payment for the Tube — much cheaper than paper tickets.

hotels.name: Apex Temple Court Hotel
hotels.location: 1-2 Serjeants' Inn, Fleet Street, EC4Y 1LL, London

hotels.name: The Goring
hotels.location: 15 Beeston Place, Grosvenor Gardens SW1W 0JW, London

hotels.name: The Zetter Hotel
hotels.neighborhood: St John's Square, 86 Clerkenwell Rd EC1M 5RJ
hotels.location: St John's Square, 86 Clerkenwell Rd EC1M 5RJ, London

hotels.name: The Trafalgar
hotels.location: 2 Spring Gdns, Trafalgar Square, SW1A 2TS, London

hotels.name: Premier Inn London Leicester Square
hotels.location: 1 Leicester Place, Leicester Square, WC2H 7BP, London

hotels.name: Portobello Hotel
hotels.location: 22 Stanley Gardens, Notting Hill W11 2NG, London

hotels.name: Twenty Navern Square Hotel
hotels.location: 20 Nevern Square, Earls Court, SW5 9PD, London
